payforge.name: PayForge
payforge.notes:
• Added Benefit in Kind (BiK) tax support across relevant calculators and pay checks.
• Added Salary Comparison to compare salaries, pay rises, and salary sacrifice scenarios.
• Improved premium gating and reset behaviour when Premium expires.
• Added iCloud sync across devices and iCloud backup and restore.
• Improved Sync & Data status reporting and backup explanations.
• Improved payday calculations, reminders, weekend, and bank holiday handling.
• Improved notification management.
• Optimised data processing and reduced unnecessary app work.
• Fixed navigation, status messaging, and SwiftUI issues.
• Improved layout and clarity throughout Settings and calculation screens.

releaseHistory.notes:
• Added Benefit in Kind (BiK) tax support across relevant calculators and pay checks.
• Added Salary Comparison to compare salaries, pay rises, and salary sacrifice scenarios.
• Improved premium gating and reset behaviour when Premium expires.
• Added iCloud sync across devices and iCloud backup and restore.
• Improved Sync & Data status reporting and backup explanations.
• Improved payday calculations, reminders, weekend, and bank holiday handling.
• Improved notification management.
• Optimised data processing and reduced unnecessary app work.
• Fixed navigation, status messaging, and SwiftUI issues.
• Improved layout and clarity throughout Settings and calculation screens.

• Added Premium Access Audit in Settings with clear entitlement status, active plan, and reset history
• Improved premium expiry handling so premium-only settings safely reset when access ends
• Refined iCloud Sync & Data with clearer sync state, health checks, and event logging
• Added clearer sync guidance for cross-device behaviour and iCloud requirements
• Added notifications options in the settings section for on device payday reminders
• Updated payday reminders to follow each profile’s next calculated payday more reliably
• Fixed Next Payday progression issues that could show incorrect negative countdowns
• Improved Check Pay premium gating for a more consistent locked/unlocked experience
• Added attachment management in Check Pay, including preview and delete support
• Added inline PDF viewing for payslip attachments so it is no longer a placeholder 
• Optimised the application for a better experience

• Added support for iCloud*
Disabled by default and can be toggled on manually in the settings of the app under "Sync & Data"
• Added in Premium Extra Tag 
• Added in Required Tag for parts of the onboarding process which are required to progress
• Added in Salary Sacrifice Pension Quick Calculator 
• Added in subtle animations on the onboarding Screen
• Added in advanced UK tax options & an NHS worker mode
• Modified the onboarding layout for how the "Set up properly" appears 
• Updated Application Loading Screen
• Updated the Student Loan layout to check boxes rather than a drop down menu
• Updated the layout of the Quick Calculations page
• Updated UI on the Work Tab
• Resolved onboarding "Continue" and "Back" buttons jumping when pressed
• Resolved some selection boxes not appearing in the correct UI style in settings
• Resolved an issue where the pay breakdown graph colours would display incorrectly
• Resolved some incorrect button redirects in app when pressing specific buttons 
• Application now syncs subscription expiry in real time
• Optimised the application to reduce heating of devices

forgeshift.name: ForgeShift
forgeshift.notes:
• Restored the Upcoming summary to include all future scheduled shifts.
• Annual leave, days off and on-call duties are now included.
• Completed hours remain focused on the current week.

releaseHistory.notes:
• Restored the Upcoming summary to include all future scheduled shifts.
• Annual leave, days off and on-call duties are now included.
• Completed hours remain focused on the current week.

• Reduced task-assignment prewarming from up to 10–12 API requests per batch to one range request.
• Moved cache encoding and disk writes onto dedicated utility queues.
• Added in-memory shift and task-assignment caches.
• Made API configuration thread-safe and removed shared encoder/decoder concurrency risks.
• Prevented stale requests from overwriting another user’s shifts or a newly selected calendar month.
• Parallelised user, team, and team-member loading.
• Prevented reminder scheduling from extending the visible shift-loading state.
• Eliminated potentially hundreds of avatar verification requests after one upload.
• Added a bounded 15-minute avatar cache to balance performance and freshness.
• Calculated calendar month data once per render instead of rebuilding it for every day cell.
• Precomputed upcoming-shift rows and section labels in one pass.
• Replaced repeated date/time formatter work with lightweight arithmetic where appropriate.
• Corrected the weekly summary so “Upcoming” only includes the current week.
• Calendar and shift-detail screens now refresh only after an actual save.
• Fixed calendar creation and template-created shifts not reliably appearing after saving.
• Parallelised team-member requests while retaining partial successful results.
• Fixed Watch and widget views showing an expired past shift as the “next” shift.
• Reworked Watch calendar indexing from roughly days × shifts work to a single shift pass.
• Prevented outdated queued Watch transfers from rolling back newer data.
• Ensured appearance updates preserve the current Watch shift payload.
• Avoided repeated widget storage writes and reloads when shift content has not changed.
• Added idempotent Watch session startup and skipped redundant persistence updates.
• Retained correct Gregorian server-date handling for devices using non-Gregorian calendars.
• Removed unused models, state, methods, formatting helpers, and the obsolete share-sheet component.

• Added in .ics exports
• Removed confirmation from "Sign Out" button
• Added support for server-side "maintenance" which can be toggled by an admin on the web version
• Fixed issues with 2FA not always remembering for 30 days when toggled
• Added support for full on-device offline cache, remembering if a user is logged in even if the server connection is fully lost, rather than signing the user out 
• Overall app optimisation and stabilisation updates

previousUpdates.notes:
• Reduced task-assignment prewarming from up to 10–12 API requests per batch to one range request.
• Moved cache encoding and disk writes onto dedicated utility queues.
• Added in-memory shift and task-assignment caches.
• Made API configuration thread-safe and removed shared encoder/decoder concurrency risks.
• Prevented stale requests from overwriting another user’s shifts or a newly selected calendar month.
• Parallelised user, team, and team-member loading.
• Prevented reminder scheduling from extending the visible shift-loading state.
• Eliminated potentially hundreds of avatar verification requests after one upload.
• Added a bounded 15-minute avatar cache to balance performance and freshness.
• Calculated calendar month data once per render instead of rebuilding it for every day cell.
• Precomputed upcoming-shift rows and section labels in one pass.
• Replaced repeated date/time formatter work with lightweight arithmetic where appropriate.
• Corrected the weekly summary so “Upcoming” only includes the current week.
• Calendar and shift-detail screens now refresh only after an actual save.
• Fixed calendar creation and template-created shifts not reliably appearing after saving.
• Parallelised team-member requests while retaining partial successful results.
• Fixed Watch and widget views showing an expired past shift as the “next” shift.
• Reworked Watch calendar indexing from roughly days × shifts work to a single shift pass.
• Prevented outdated queued Watch transfers from rolling back newer data.
• Ensured appearance updates preserve the current Watch shift payload.
• Avoided repeated widget storage writes and reloads when shift content has not changed.
• Added idempotent Watch session startup and skipped redundant persistence updates.
• Retained correct Gregorian server-date handling for devices using non-Gregorian calendars.
• Removed unused models, state, methods, formatting helpers, and the obsolete share-sheet component.

• Added in .ics exports
• Removed confirmation from "Sign Out" button
• Added support for server-side "maintenance" which can be toggled by an admin on the web version
• Fixed issues with 2FA not always remembering for 30 days when toggled
• Added support for full on-device offline cache, remembering if a user is logged in even if the server connection is fully lost, rather than signing the user out 
• Overall app optimisation and stabilisation updates

watchcue.name: WatchCue
watchcue.notes:
• Redesigned series pages around seasons instead of displaying episodes immediately.
• Added season cards showing watched episodes, total episodes, and progress.
• Added quick watched/unwatched controls to every season.
• Added dedicated season pages containing individual episode lists.
• Moved season and whole-series bulk watch actions into the season area.
• Next-episode suggestions now show every eligible series instead of stopping after five.
• Next-episode suggestions are limited to shows marked as Watching.
• Planning, On Hold, Dropped, Caught Up, and Completed shows are excluded from next-episode suggestions.
• Added VoiceOver labels identifying the season affected by quick actions.
• Added German, Spanish, and French translations for the new season interface.
• Matched bottom spacing between series pages and Upcoming.
• Standardised edge spacing across one-, two-, and three-column Profile layouts.
• Fixed season pages briefly opening and immediately returning to series details.
• Fixed episode details appearing briefly before returning to the season page.
• Fixed Profile grid navigation reopening series details over season pages.
• Fixed Profile grid cards displaying unwanted disclosure arrows.
• Fixed Profile grid cards opening the last show instead of the selected show.
• Fixed watched-state changes becoming stale after returning from episode details.
• Fixed obsolete episode metadata affecting season totals and progress.
• Fixed duplicate weekday identifiers for Tuesday/Thursday and Saturday/Sunday in the Upcoming calendar.
• Reduced undefined SwiftUI grid behaviour and repeated layout updates that could contribute to interface lock-ups.
• Unified series, season, and episode navigation under one value-based navigation path.
• Made season pages query persisted episode information independently.
• Preserved stable season-route identity while filtering against current episode IDs.
• Removed obsolete captured bulk-action and navigation state.
• Added coverage for next-episode eligibility and Remaining-count behaviour.

releaseHistory.notes:
• Redesigned series pages around seasons instead of displaying episodes immediately.
• Added season cards showing watched episodes, total episodes, and progress.
• Added quick watched/unwatched controls to every season.
• Added dedicated season pages containing individual episode lists.
• Moved season and whole-series bulk watch actions into the season area.
• Next-episode suggestions now show every eligible series instead of stopping after five.
• Next-episode suggestions are limited to shows marked as Watching.
• Planning, On Hold, Dropped, Caught Up, and Completed shows are excluded from next-episode suggestions.
• Added VoiceOver labels identifying the season affected by quick actions.
• Added German, Spanish, and French translations for the new season interface.
• Matched bottom spacing between series pages and Upcoming.
• Standardised edge spacing across one-, two-, and three-column Profile layouts.
• Fixed season pages briefly opening and immediately returning to series details.
• Fixed episode details appearing briefly before returning to the season page.
• Fixed Profile grid navigation reopening series details over season pages.
• Fixed Profile grid cards displaying unwanted disclosure arrows.
• Fixed Profile grid cards opening the last show instead of the selected show.
• Fixed watched-state changes becoming stale after returning from episode details.
• Fixed obsolete episode metadata affecting season totals and progress.
• Fixed duplicate weekday identifiers for Tuesday/Thursday and Saturday/Sunday in the Upcoming calendar.
• Reduced undefined SwiftUI grid behaviour and repeated layout updates that could contribute to interface lock-ups.
• Unified series, season, and episode navigation under one value-based navigation path.
• Made season pages query persisted episode information independently.
• Preserved stable season-route identity while filtering against current episode IDs.
• Removed obsolete captured bulk-action and navigation state.
• Added coverage for next-episode eligibility and Remaining-count behaviour.

• Added Device Language support with optional manual language overrides.
• Added English, Spanish, French and German localisations across onboarding, settings, navigation, statuses, errors and empty states.
• Fixed language switching, persistence and English fallback behaviour.
• Added translated TV and movie metadata with English fallback.
• Added metadata language and region selectors.
• Added one, two and three-column Profile layouts, with one column as the default.
• Improved Profile grid card sizing, spacing, typography and Dynamic Type support.
• Fixed Profile grid navigation, unwanted arrows, disappearing toolbar controls and excessive back-navigation.
• Moved Library and Profile layout controls into Customisation settings.
• Reset Customisation now restores Library and Profile layout defaults.
• Improved search ordering, stability, history layout and caching.
• Added offline caching for artwork, library metadata, search results and viewing data.
• Improved artwork loading and cache-clearing reliability.
• Added cache storage information, individual clearing controls and cache-clearing warnings.
• Added information about the 250 MB artwork cache limit.
• Reduced unnecessary API calls, metadata refreshes, data lookups and SwiftData queries.
• Added bounded API caches and improved cache concurrency and memory management.
• Improved Home, Search, Library, Profile, Upcoming and title-detail loading performance.
• Added stable sorting to prevent content unexpectedly reordering after refreshes.
• Dropped shows no longer count towards episode progress or remaining episodes.
• Improved Caught Up and Completed status calculations for continuing and ended shows.
• Shows are now automatically followed when an episode is marked as watched.
• Added reversible season and all-season watched actions with updated labels.
• Improved manual episode marking, immediate checkmark feedback and gesture handling.
• Improved movie watched/unwatched actions, ratings and Profile filter switching.
• Added calendar release indicators and quick release-day filtering.
• Improved Home “Up Next” artwork, long-title layouts, episode details and release dates.
• Fixed landscape episode artwork overlapping Home screen content.
• Improved Settings organisation, interaction feedback and accessibility.
• Improved CloudKit backup handling, validation and error reporting.
• Improved account deletion and private CloudKit backup cleanup.
• Backup imports now reject duplicate episode and movie records before changing local data.
• Corrected App Store donation product identifiers.
• Removed unused and duplicated code and expanded regression coverage.
• General stability, performance, caching and memory-usage improvements throughout the app.
